sections.topic: Language: Skill, Tool, Form, and Function

pre_reading.question: What is language?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Language is a skill, a tool for communication, a form of verbal and non-verbal expression, and a means to perform social functions.

pre_reading.question: What do we use it for?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: We use it to communicate with others, share information, express our feelings, ask questions, and perform daily social tasks.

pre_reading.question: What does language do for us?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Language helps us connect with people, reflects and preserves our culture, passes traditions to the next generation, and defines our social identity.

pre_reading.question: What would our life be like without language?
pre_reading.suggested_answer: Without language, our life would be incredibly difficult because we would not be able to share complex ideas, express thoughts clearly, or build organized societies.

paragraphs.english_text: The question, 'What is language?' can have several different answers, and all of them may be acceptable. For our present purpose, however, let us look at language as: skill, a tool, a form, and a function.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဘာသာစကားဆိုတာ ဘာလဲဆိုတဲ့ မေးခွန်းအတွက် လက်ခံနိုင်တဲ့ အဖြေများ ရှိပါတယ်။ ယခု သင်ခန်းစာအတွက် ဘာသာစကားကို စွမ်းရည်၊ ကိရိယာ၊ ပုံသဏ္ဌာန်၊ နှင့် လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်အဖြစ် ယူဆကြပါစို့။

paragraphs.english_text: First, language is a set of skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing. They are often known as the four language skills. Of these four skills, the listening skill is the first to develop.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ပထမအချက်အနေနဲ့ ဘာသာစကားဟာ နားထောင်ခြင်း၊ ပြောဆိုခြင်း၊ ဖတ်ခြင်း၊ နှင့် ရေးသားခြင်း စွမ်းရည်များ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Here, we should note that listening and speaking work together as a pair of skills, while reading and writing work together as another pair. We should note further that when a baby is born, the baby listens first, then speaks.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: မှတ်သားရမှာက နားထောင်ခြင်းနဲ့ ပြောဆိုခြင်းက တွဲလျက် စွမ်းရည်တစ်ခုအဖြစ် လုပ်ဆောင်တယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Second, language is a tool for communication. This means that we use language to speak to someone, or to write to someone. To speak, we use vocal symbols; to write, we use graphic symbols such as letters and words.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဒုတိယအချက်ကတော့ ဘာသာစကားဟာ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးအတွက် ကိရိယာတစ်ခု ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Third, communication may be in one of two forms: verbal communication, and non-verbal communication. In verbal communication, we use words and sentences. They may be spoken or written.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: တတိယအချက်အနေနဲ့ ဆက်သွယ်ရေးမှာ ပုံသဏ္ဌာန်နှစ်မျိုး ရှိပါတယ်။

paragraphs.english_text: Fourth, we use language to help us perform many social functions, which are very much part of our day-to-day life. This means that we use language: to greet or to bid farewell, to request, to direct, to thank, to apologize, and to agree or disagree.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: စတုတ္ထအချက်ကတော့ ဘာသာစကားကို နေ့စဉ်လူမှုဘဝ လုပ်ဆောင်ချက်များ သုံးရန်ကြောင်း။

paragraphs.english_text: In addition, language reflects culture, preserves culture, passes on culture from one generation to the next, preserves nationality, and also identifies social class.
paragraphs.burmese_explanation: ဒါ့အပြင် ဘာသာစကားဟာ ယဉ်ကျေးမှုကို ထင်ဟပ်စေတယ်။

part_A.instructions: Complete each sentence below with a word or phrase from the reading passage.

exercises.text: The four language skills are: ___________.
The first language skill to develop is ___________.
A baby begins to speak at the age of ___________.
exercises.answer: about one and a half years or so

exercises.text: Listening and ___________ work together as a pair of skills.
The other pair of skills includes ___________.
Speaking and writing are ___________ skills.
Language is also known as a ___________ for communication.
The two forms of communication are ___________ and ___________.
We use ___________ when we speak to help the listener understand better.
We use ___________ when we write to help the reader understand better.

part_B.instructions: Give a short answer to each question below.

exercises.question: When does a child begin to read and write?
exercises.answer: Only when the child goes to school.

exercises.question: What are the productive skills of language?
What are the receptive skills of language?
Why do we use gestures when we speak?
exercises.answer: To help the listener understand us better.

exercises.question: How do we help the reader understand what we write better?
exercises.answer: By using graphics such as pictures, photographs, charts, and tables.

exercises.question: What are the two forms of communication?
Do you want to learn any other foreign language apart from English? Why?
exercises.answer: Yes, I want to learn Japanese because I want to study abroad and it will help me communicate better.

exercises.question: Which language skill is the most difficult for you to learn? Why?
exercises.answer: Writing is the most difficult skill for me because it requires a lot of practice and careful attention to grammar and spelling.

part_C.instructions: Write the function of each utterance within the brackets given.

exercises.utterance: May I use the phone, please?
It's very kind of you to help me.
Could you please scan the photo for me?
Go along this street and turn right.
exercises.function: directing someone how to get to a certain place
responding to words of thanks and gratitude

exercises.utterance: I don't quite agree with what you said.
Make three copies of this article.

part_A.instructions: Complete the table. Australia and China are done for you as examples.

part_B.instructions: Rewrite each sentence below, filling the blanks with appropriate words from the table in Exercise A.

exercises.text: Because I speak ___________ fluently, I did not have much trouble in France.
Those tourists are from Italy and they speak only ___________ and do not understand a word of English.
I wish to visit Vietnam but I do not speak a word of ___________.
Since you speak ___________ quite well, you should have no problem studying in the USA.
I plan to work in Korea, so I'm learning ___________ at the Yangon University of Foreign Languages.
A kimono is a traditional ___________ garment.
William Shakespeare is a very widely known ___________ playwright and poet.
I have never been to Laos and I have never tried ___________ food.
He grew up in Germany and he speaks ___________ fluently.
I like ___________ food though I don't know how to use the chopstick.

part_A.instructions: Spot the nouns in apposition in the following sentences and underline them.

exercises.text: There are many pagodas in Bagan, an ancient capital of Myanmar.
The ostrich, a kind of flightless bird, is found only in Africa.
My son, a musician, earns little and lives with me.
Loch Ness, a large mountain lake, is in Scotland.
Mt. Everest, the highest peak in the world, is in Nepal.
apposition_phrases: the highest peak in the world

exercises.text: I'd love to climb Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Tanzania.
Brussels sprout, a green vegetable like a tiny cabbage, is quite delicious to eat.
apposition_phrases: a green vegetable like a tiny cabbage

exercises.text: The Nile, the longest river in the world, is in northeastern Africa.
apposition_phrases: the longest river in the world

exercises.text: Judo, a Japanese martial art, originated from jujitsu, a samurai unarmed fighting technique.
